title: Vivian — first-adventure rescue (Stage 2 — the bond)
prompt: Three serpent-cult footmen. Narrow stone corridor. No back-step. The hero squares his stance — and a knife flicks past from the dark above and takes the front man in the throat. Vivian drops from a ledge above, lands light as a cat, plants her short sword in the second man's kidney before he turns. "Don't just stand there, soldier." The third she leaves for the hero. After — she crouches at a small iron-bound chest, picks the lock in a dozen heartbeats, lifts out a healing potion and tosses it to him without looking. "You looked thirsty." Then she's gone again, into the next chamber, scouting ahead.

choices.label: Thank her, sincerely
choices.resolutionHint: She catches the thank-you over her shoulder, smiles to herself in the dark, and keeps walking. He has the potion. She has the look on his face. Bond formed.

choices.label: Pocket the potion without comment, follow her
choices.resolutionHint: He pockets the potion and falls in behind her. No words. She'll respect that — she's the same way. Bond formed, slightly cooler in tone.

choices.label: Pretend not to remember her name
choices.resolutionHint: He says "What was your name again?" Her smile flickers. "Vivian. The girl who just saved your life." She's gone before he can correct it. The bond forms anyway — she finishes the run with him — but a small cold thing has been deposited that won't entirely leave.
